description: Revised competitive matrix that broadens the market model beyond Harvest's trust/governance moat. This v2 adds browser infrastructure, anti-bot, no-code extraction, platform ecosystem, dataset, and monitoring dimensions so category leaders are not artificially underrated.

methodology.scoring_rule: Scores reflect working, user-accessible capability today. Code that exists but is not wired into the main path is discounted. Hosted platforms are allowed to score highly on operational dimensions even if Harvest prefers local-first architecture.
major_v2_changes: Lowered several DanteHarvest scores where recent gains are partial rather than fully integrated.
Added dimensions for browser infrastructure, anti-bot, proxy networks, scheduling, datasets, platform ecosystem, no-code UX, and structured extraction APIs.
Expanded the competitor universe to include the major commercial and OSS scraping/browser tools discussed in the PRD review.

dimensions.label: Evidence Chain Robustness
dimensions.note: Still a real moat.

dimensions.label: Rights Model Completeness
dimensions.note: Artifact-level rights modeling remains unusual in the market.

dimensions.label: Pack Promotion Pipeline
dimensions.note: Still unusually strong.

dimensions.label: Vector Search Integration
dimensions.note: Phase 8: hybrid BM25+dense retrieval with configurable alpha and optional cross-encoder reranking. Not 9 because O(n) scan rather than HNSW ANN.

dimensions.label: Replay Harness Fidelity
dimensions.note: Cycle 5: ActionType enum (11 types), from_action() parser, action_handlers Dict routing in ReplayHarness — type-specific dispatch before falling back to step_executor.

dimensions.label: Redaction Accuracy
dimensions.note: Cycle 2: pii_patterns.py — 16 new patterns (Stripe/SendGrid/Google/npm keys, IBAN, intl phone, EIN, passport, VIN, Medicare). register_extended_patterns() wired into scanner.

dimensions.label: CLI Completeness
dimensions.note: Solid but not category-leading.

dimensions.label: Observation Plane Depth
dimensions.note: Still far behind continuous capture systems.

dimensions.label: Taxonomy Builder
dimensions.note: Useful but still relatively basic.

dimensions.label: Crawl Acquisition
dimensions.note: Phase 0+9: JS rendering path fully wired, stealth headers, proxy passthrough. Not 8 because no proxy network or Cloudflare bypass.

dimensions.label: Transcription Quality
dimensions.note: Batch-first, not continuous.

dimensions.label: Constitutional Doctrine Enforcement
dimensions.note: Still unique.

dimensions.label: Test Coverage
dimensions.note: 501 passing unit tests across all 10 build phases. Not 9 because no integration or e2e test layer.

dimensions.label: DanteAgents Integration
dimensions.note: Strong internal ecosystem advantage.

dimensions.label: Local-First Guarantee
dimensions.note: Strong differentiator, though not always a market-leading buying criterion.

dimensions.label: AGPL Quarantine
dimensions.note: Important governance advantage, low broad-market weight.

dimensions.label: Multi-Format Ingest
dimensions.note: Phase 7: added GitHub, Notion, S3 connectors on top of existing file/URL/EPUB/XLSX/crawl. Not 9 because missing Slack, GDrive, Confluence.

dimensions.label: UI / Reviewer Workflow
dimensions.note: Phase 1: React SPA with 4 tabs (Pending/Deferred/Approved/Rejected), confidence badges, approve/reject/defer wired to state machine, 5s polling. Not 9 because no SSO or role-based access.

dimensions.label: Specialization Pack Quality
dimensions.note: Promising schema; scale and versioning still immature.

dimensions.label: Eval Harness
dimensions.note: Improved, but still not a fully mature eval product.

dimensions.label: Anti-Bot Bypass
dimensions.note: Phase 9: 20-UA rotation pool, Sec-Ch-Ua/Sec-Fetch-* stealth headers, Retry-After respect, proxy_url passthrough. Ceiling ~4 without proxy network infrastructure â€” documented in PRIME.md.

dimensions.label: Proxy Network Depth
dimensions.note: Harvest does not compete here yet.

dimensions.label: Browser Infrastructure
dimensions.note: Cycle 4: DEVICE_PROFILES (5 UA+viewport+timezone fingerprints), fingerprint_profile param (None/random/named), health() per-browser metrics, error_count on _BrowserSlot.

dimensions.label: Remote Browser Scalability
dimensions.note: Also a major missing category in v1.

dimensions.label: Trace and Debug Tooling
dimensions.note: Phase 4: SessionTracer (structured JSONL events + screenshots), PlaywrightTraceCapture (.zip archive on failure), trace_path in ReplayReport, GET /api/replays/{id}/trace endpoint. Not 9 because no embedded trace viewer UI.

dimensions.label: Session Replay Debuggability
dimensions.note: Phase 4: SessionTracer wired into ReplayHarness; step-by-step event recording, automatic trace-on-failure, trace_path in ReplayReport. Not 9 because no cloud-hosted replay viewing or diff comparison.

dimensions.label: Platform Ecosystem
dimensions.note: Actors, store, storage, scheduler, and APIs matter.

dimensions.label: Job Scheduling and Storage
dimensions.note: Phase 5: HarvestScheduler with APScheduler+SQLite, cron validation, file-backed ScheduleStore, CLI add/list/remove/run-now. Not 8 because no web UI for schedule management.

dimensions.label: Structured Extraction Endpoints
dimensions.note: Phase 2: POST /scrape (sync), POST /extract (async+LLM schema), POST /crawl (async), GET /jobs/{id}, file-backed JobStore. Not 8 because no domain-specific endpoints or proxy rotation.

dimensions.label: No-Code Extraction UX
dimensions.note: Not currently a Harvest category.

dimensions.label: Dataset Marketplace
dimensions.note: Ready-made data products are a distinct commercial advantage.

dimensions.label: Connector Breadth
dimensions.note: Cycle 6: PostgresConnector (psycopg2 optional, schema introspection, markdown tables) and GitLabConnector (REST API v4, paginated tree, file content ingestion, extension filtering).

dimensions.label: Agent Browser Infrastructure
dimensions.note: Cycle 1: ActionLayer with 11 typed action types, BrowserAction/ActionResult dataclasses, pre/post DOM snapshots, element existence checks, fail-closed error handling.

dimensions.label: Monitoring and Alerting
dimensions.note: Cycle 3: AlertRule threshold engine, dedup_window_secs suppression, dead_letters queue (maxlen=200), add/remove/list rules. Production-grade event management.

competitors.name: DanteHarvest
competitors.core_identity: Trust-heavy harvest / governance substrate with React reviewer UI, hybrid vector search, browser pool, job scheduler, event bus, connectors, and structured extraction API. Architectural leader on trust; competitive on most operational dimensions.

competitors.name: Bright Data
competitors.core_identity: Enterprise web data platform centered on difficult-site access, proxy scale, and data products.

competitors.name: Apify
competitors.core_identity: Actor platform and marketplace for scraping and automation workflows.

competitors.name: Playwright
competitors.core_identity: Best-in-class browser automation primitive layer.

competitors.name: Browserless
competitors.core_identity: Managed browser operations platform for Playwright and Puppeteer users.

competitors.name: LlamaIndex
competitors.core_identity: Retrieval and connector platform with deep ecosystem maturity.

competitors.name: Hyperbrowser
competitors.core_identity: Stateful cloud browser infrastructure for agents.

competitors.name: Microsoft Recall
competitors.core_identity: OS-level recall and memory with deep capture access.

competitors.name: Rewind.ai
competitors.core_identity: Polished memory UX with high user-facing quality.

competitors.name: Firecrawl
competitors.core_identity: AI-friendly web extraction and crawl-to-markdown pipeline.

competitors.name: ScraperAPI
competitors.core_identity: Simple scraping API with rendering, proxy abstraction, and domain-specific endpoints.

competitors.name: Screenpipe
competitors.core_identity: Continuous local observation and searchable capture history.

competitors.name: Haystack
competitors.core_identity: Retrieval and evaluation stack with strong NLP components.

competitors.name: Scrapy
competitors.core_identity: Classic programmable crawler framework with very strong spider architecture.

competitors.name: Browse AI
competitors.core_identity: Business-user scraping monitors and workflow automation.

competitors.name: OpenAdapt
competitors.core_identity: Record/replay-inspired automation workflow toolkit.

competitors.name: Octoparse
competitors.core_identity: No-code visual extractor for business users.

competitors.name: LangChain
competitors.core_identity: Large orchestration ecosystem with broad familiarity.

competitors.name: Webscraper.io
competitors.core_identity: Accessible browser-extension and cloud scraping workflow.

competitors.name: Paperless-ngx
competitors.core_identity: Strong document workflow and browser review UX.

competitors.name: ScrapingBee
competitors.core_identity: Developer-friendly scraping API with JS rendering and proxy rotation.

competitors.name: Stagehand
competitors.core_identity: Higher-level browser actions for AI agents.

competitors.name: Browser Use
competitors.core_identity: Agent-facing browser automation abstraction.

competitors.name: Skyvern
competitors.core_identity: Managed web task automation product.

competitors.name: Aider
competitors.core_identity: Excellent terminal-centered developer interaction loop.

competitors.name: Crawl4AI
competitors.core_identity: Accessible AI-first open crawling and extraction.

competitors.name: Notion AI
competitors.core_identity: Strong knowledge work UX, weak trust/governance substrate.

competitors.name: Cursor
competitors.core_identity: Strong AI coding product UX.

competitors.name: AutoGen
competitors.core_identity: Agent runtime and orchestration framework.

competitors.name: OpenRecall
competitors.core_identity: Recall-style local memory project.

competitors.name: CrewAI
competitors.core_identity: Multi-agent workflow framework.

competitors.name: Mem.ai
competitors.core_identity: Memory UX product rather than infrastructure platform.

competitors.name: Instructor
competitors.core_identity: Very strong structured generation helper, narrow category coverage.

ceiling_dimensions: dataset_marketplace (ceiling 3 â€” requires curation and hosting)
no_code_extraction_ux (ceiling 5 â€” requires significant visual UX work)
meta.interpretation: DanteHarvest closed 13 dimension gaps across 10 build phases (+0.94 weighted average). Now competitive vs Bright Data (7.18) on trust dimensions, competitive vs Apify on scheduling, and ahead of Firecrawl on crawl pipeline integration. Remaining structural ceiling: proxy network and cloud browser scale require infrastructure investment beyond local-first scope.
